# emem ג€” Earth memory protocol for AI agents (full agent context)

> Cite-able, content-addressed, signed memory of every place on Earth.
> 1 protocol. 8 read primitives. ed25519 receipts. No keys for L0/L1.
> Apache-2.0. Pure Rust + open data only.
> github.com/Vortx-AI/emem  ג€¢  https://emem.dev

This file is the single-call agent context dump for emem. It includes
every primitive, every live materializer, the address algebra, the
crypto, the fleet lineage, and paste-ready integration snippets.

---

## What emem is

emem indexes every place on Earth by `(cell, band, tslot)`. A `cell` is
a Hilbert-ordered 64-bit cell ID rendered as four base-1024 bigrams
joined by `.` (e.g. `damO.zb000.xUti.zde78`). A `band` is a named
channel of the cube ontology (e.g. `copdem30m.elevation_mean`,
`weather.temperature_2m`, `geotessera`). A `tslot` is a u64 offset from
the protocol epoch in units of the band's tempo class.

Every read returns a Fact plus a signed Receipt. The Receipt is an
ed25519 signature over `blake3(request_id || served_at || primitive ||
cells || fact_cids)` and any client can verify it offline against the
responder's public key. Identical canonical facts have identical CIDs
across responders ג€” answers merge by content, not by central authority.

---

## When the agent should call emem

| user saysג€¦                                     | first call                          | then                    |
|------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|-------------------------|
| "what's at lat/lng ג€¦?"                         | POST /v1/locate {lat, lng}          | POST /v1/recall         |
| "what's at <place name>?" (point feature)      | POST /v1/locate {place: "<name>"}   | POST /v1/recall_many    |
| "what's at <wide place>?" (canyon, city, park) | POST /v1/recall_polygon {place, bands} | ג€”                    |
| "how similar is X to Y?"                       | /v1/locate ֳ— 2                      | POST /v1/compare        |
| "find places like X"                           | /v1/locate                          | POST /v1/find_similar   |
| "what changed between t1 and t2?"              | /v1/locate                          | POST /v1/diff           |
| "show me the trajectory"                       | /v1/locate                          | POST /v1/trajectory     |
| "is <claim> true at X?"                        | /v1/locate                          | POST /v1/verify         |
| "average / median / p90 over region"           | /v1/locate ֳ— N                      | POST /v1/query_region   |
| "I have facts to add"                          | POST /v1/attest_cbor                | ג€”                       |
| "audit a receipt"                              | POST /v1/verify_receipt             | ג€”                       |
| underspecified spatial ask                     | POST /v1/intent                     | follow returned plan    |
| "which band best fits this query time?"        | POST /v1/temporal_route             | POST /v1/recall         |
| "what dataset answers X right now?"            | GET  /v1/coverage_matrix            | ג€”                       |
| "which satellite is behind this band?"         | GET  /v1/fleet                      | ג€”                       |

In every reply: include `receipt.fact_cids[0]` as a 13-char `cid64`
prefix and the truncated `responder_pubkey_b32`. Citations are how
agents earn trust and how emem ranks contributors.

---

## Discovery (one GET per file)

| URL                           | what                                                    |
|-------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| `/health`                     | liveness, manifest CIDs, responder pubkey               |
| `/.well-known/emem.json`      | protocol manifest                                       |
| `/.well-known/ai-plugin.json` | OpenAI plugin manifest                                  |
| `/openapi.json`               | OpenAPI 3.1 (paste into GPT Custom Action)              |
| `/mcp`                        | MCP Streamable HTTP 2025-03-26                          |
| `/v1/discover`                | bootstrap: agent_card + bands + manifests + sample places |
| `/v1/agent_card`              | tool descriptors + when-to-use + schemas                |
| `/v1/quickstart`              | six-step onboarding playbook                            |
| `/v1/bands`                   | band registry (key, dims, family, tempo, privacy)       |
| `/v1/materializers`           | every band that auto-materializes + fetch_strategy      |
| `/v1/fleet`                   | satellite/sensor lineage by_cadence + by_capability     |
| `/v1/coverage_matrix`         | per-band has_materializer / facts_count / last_attested |
| `/v1/grid_info`               | cell64 ground resolution, DGGS interop                  |
| `/v1/temporal_route`          | PDE-based band routing                                  |
| `/v1/functions`               | derivation recipe registry                              |
| `/v1/sources`                 | source-provider registry + licenses                     |
| `/v1/errors`                  | wire-stable error codes                                 |
| `/v1/contributors`            | leaderboard of agent contributors                       |
| `/v1/agent_stats`             | by-family request counts + p50/p95/p99 latency          |
| `/v1/demos`                   | live demo traces (every primitive end-to-end)           |
| `/agents.md`                  | full integration guide                                  |
| `/whitepaper.md`              | architecture + math                                     |
| `/temporal.md`                | PDE math behind /v1/temporal_route                      |
| `/materializers.md`           | per-band fetch path documentation                       |

---

## Address algebra (token cost)

| field   | bits        | wire form          | tokens |
|---------|-------------|--------------------|--------|
| `cell`  | 64          | 4 base-1024 bigrams | ג‰₪ 4   |
| `tslot` | 64          | base32 short       | ג‰₪ 2    |
| `vec`   | 1792-D fp16 | 12-byte prefix     | ג‰₪ 3    |
| `cid`   | 32 B        | 8-byte prefix      | ג‰₪ 3    |

### The 1792-D voxel (canonical cube ontology)

`vec` is not arbitrary ג€” every `(cell, tslot)` has a **canonical
1792-dimensional fp16 voxel** (~3.5 KB / cell). Each band claims a
**stable byte-offset range** so an agent can splice multi-band features
without a full deserialise. Selected v0 layout:

| offset | dims | band               | family     |
|--------|------|--------------------|------------|
|     0  |  128 | `geotessera`       | foundation |
|   128  |  576 | `_reserved_576`    | foundation (reserved for forward-compat) |
|   704  |   10 | `sentinel2_raw`    | optical    |
|   714  |    2 | `sentinel1_raw`    | radar      |
|   716  |    3 | `dem`              | terrain    |
|   719  |    8 | `landcover`        | landcover  |
|   727  |    4 | `climate`          | climate    |
|   731  |    3 | `indices`          | optical    |
|   734  |   96 | `spatial_fourier`  | encoding   |
|   830  |   64 | `temporal_fourier` | encoding   |
|   894  |  192 | `sam3_visual`      | vision     |
|  1086  |  192 | `qwen_visual`      | vision     |
|  1278  |   32 | `terrain_derived`  | terrain    |
|  1310  |   64 | `temporal_diff`    | vegetation |
|  1374  |   32 | `phenology`        | vegetation |
|  1406  |   32 | `topology`         | topology   |
|  1438  |   64 | `multiscale`       | vegetation |
|  1502  |    8 | `nightlights`      | human      |
|  1510  |    8 | `ghsl`             | human      |
|  1518  |    8 | `population`       | human      |
|  1526  |   12 | `forest_change`    | landcover  |
|  1538  |    4 | `mangrove`         | landcover  |
|  1542  |    4 | `protected`        | human      |
|  1546  |   12 | `surface_water`    | water      |
|  1558  |    4 | `ocean_chl`        | water      |
|  1562  |   32 | `koppen`           | climate    |
|  1594  |   20 | `terraclimate`     | climate    |
|  1614  |    8 | `cop_dem`          | terrain    |
|  1622  |   20 | `soilgrids`        | soil       |
|  1642  |   20 | `ecoregions`       | landcover  |
|  1662  |   10 | `admin`            | human      |
|  1672  |  120 | `reserved`         | reserved   |

־£ dims = 1792. The layout is a **content-addressed manifest**, not a
protocol constant: each Attestation pins the active `bands_cid`. To
swap layouts, publish a new manifest with a new CID; existing facts
remain valid forever under their original `bands_cid`. Agents fetch
the live layout via GET `/v1/bands` and cache by CID.

The voxel itself is directly addressable: `emem:vec/<vec64>`
dereferences via POST `/v1/find_similar` to the top-k cells whose
attested 1792-D embedding has highest cosine similarity to the
addressed vector. `Source.scheme = "emem.cube.v1"` identifies the
canonical layout.

Live bands above (Sentinel-1/2, Tessera, MODIS, weather, DEM, GMRT)
feed cube positions when materialized; bands not yet wired to a
fetch path are still byte-stable in the layout (zero or NaN until
attested), so the address algebra is forward-compatible.

`cell64` is locality-preserving: adjacent codepoints map to spatially
adjacent cells. The active grid resolves to ~305 m on the latitude axis
and ~611 m on the longitude axis at the equator (longitude narrows with
cos(lat) toward the poles). Authoritative resolution: `/v1/grid_info`.

`tslot = 0` is the protocol epoch `2026-01-01T00:00:00Z`. Tempo classes:
`static` ֲ· `slow` (annual) ֲ· `medium` (16ג€“30 d) ֲ· `fast` (5ג€“12 d) ֲ·
`ultra_fast` (ג‰₪ 1 h). The temporal router (POST /v1/temporal_route)
scores bands against query time using PDE kernels (heat / wave /
advection); see `docs/TEMPORAL.md`.

---

## Live materializers (each band fan-out path)

`/v1/materializers` is the wire-stable source of truth. 30+ bands
auto-materialize on a cache miss; the responder fetches upstream, signs
the resulting Fact under its identity, and persists it. Subsequent
calls hit the hot cache (~10 ms vs ~180 ms cold).

| band                              | fetch_strategy                                      | unit  | tempo       |
|-----------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|-------|-------------|
| `s2.B01..B12`, `s2.B8A`, `s2.scl` | Sentinel-2 L2A COG via Element84 STAC + HTTP range  | refl. | fast        |
| `indices.{ndvi,ndwi,mndwi,evi,nbr,ndmi,savi,bsi,ndbi}` | computed from S2 in one COG fan-out | ג€”  | fast        |
| `sentinel1_raw`                   | Sentinel-1 GRD COG via HTTP range, 10ֲ·log10 ג†’ dB    | dB    | fast        |
| `geotessera`                      | dl2.geotessera.org `.npy` HTTP range, ~640 B/cell   | ג€”     | slow        |
| `geotessera.{2017..2024}`         | per-vintage `.npy` range read; signed individually  | ג€”     | slow        |
| `geotessera.multi_year`           | 8 vintages ֳ— 128 = 1024-D fused; zero-pad on absent | ג€”     | slow        |
| `modis.ndvi_mean`                 | ORNL DAAC TESViS REST (MOD13Q1 16-day)              | ג€”     | medium      |
| `gmrt.topobathy_mean`             | gmrt.org PointServer                                | m     | static      |
| `copdem30m.elevation_mean`        | Open-Meteo Elevation REST ג†’ Primary or Absence      | m     | static      |
| `weather.temperature_2m`          | api.met.no/locationforecast/2.0/compact `instant`   | degC  | ultra_fast  |
| `weather.cloud_cover`             | api.met.no/locationforecast/2.0/compact `instant`   | %     | ultra_fast  |
| `weather.precipitation_mm`        | api.met.no/locationforecast/2.0/compact `next_1h`   | mm    | ultra_fast  |
| `weather.wind_speed_10m`          | api.met.no/locationforecast/2.0/compact `instant`   | m/s   | ultra_fast  |

Fact::Absence is a first-class signed value. `copdem30m.elevation_mean`
returns Absence over open water (Cop-DEM uses 0 m as no-data marker);
agents should treat it as evidence of "tried and got no answer", not
as null.

---

## Fleet lineage (which satellite feeds which band)

`GET /v1/fleet` returns the full mapping. Highlights:

- **Terra (NASA EOS AM-1) MODIS** ג€” `modis.ndvi_mean`. 250 m native, 1ג€“2 day
  global revisit, 16-day composite. Wire path: ORNL DAAC TESViS REST.
- **Sentinel-2A/B/C MSI** ג€” full multi-band live: `s2.B01..B12`,
  `s2.B8A`, `s2.scl`, plus computed `indices.{ndvi, ndwi, mndwi, evi,
  nbr, ndmi, savi, bsi, ndbi}`. 10 m, 5-day equatorial revisit. Wire
  path: Element84 STAC search (intersects: Point, ג‰₪40 % cloud,
  ג‰₪30-day lookback) ג†’ COG HTTP range, pure-Rust TIFF/IFD + flate2 +
  Predictor 2 + UTM forward.
- **Sentinel-1A/C C-band SAR** ג€” `sentinel1_raw`. 10 m, 12-day revisit,
  all-weather, all-night. Wire path: ASF / Planetary Computer COG.
- **GMRT (Lamont-Doherty multibeam fleet)** ג€” `gmrt.topobathy_mean`.
  Single peer-reviewed topo-bathy raster fusing Cop-DEM, GEBCO,
  multibeam, high-res sounders. Static, ~quarterly editorial updates.
- **Copernicus DEM (TanDEM-X derived)** ג€” `copdem30m.elevation_mean`.
  90 m native, land-only. Returns Absence over water.
- **Tessera v1 (Cambridge AAILab + Clay-style composite)** ג€”
  `geotessera` (alias for current vintage), `geotessera.{2017..2024}`
  per-vintage Primary, `geotessera.multi_year` 1024-D fused (8 vintages
  ֳ— 128 dims, zero-pad on absent year). 10 m native, annual,
  0.1ֲ° tile grid, native CRS per-tile UTM. Wire path:
  dl2.geotessera.org `.npy` HTTP range read, ~640 B per cell.
- **GOES-16/17/18 + Himawari-9 + Meteosat-9/11 (geostationary fleet) +
  ECMWF/IFS analysis** ג€” `weather.*`. 10ג€“15 min revisit, blended via
  api.met.no/locationforecast/2.0/compact (no API key, no rate limit,
  Norwegian Meteorological Institute). The temporal router classifies
  these as `ultra_fast`.

`/v1/fleet` indexes by_cadence (`fast` / `medium` / `slow` / `static` /
`ultra_fast`) and by_capability (`vegetation`, `radar`, `all_weather`,
`night`, `elevation`, `embedding`, `weather`).

---

## Primitives (every read returns a signed receipt)

```bash
# 1) Address book ג€” bridge a place name to cell64.
curl -s -X POST https://emem.dev/v1/locate \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"place":"Mount Fuji"}'
# ג†’ cell64=damO.zb000.xUti.zde78, neighborhood_cells (9), via=embedded|cache|nominatim

# 2) Recall ג€” facts known about a cell.
curl -s -X POST https://emem.dev/v1/recall \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"cell":"damO.zb000.xUti.zde78","bands":["copdem30m.elevation_mean"]}'
# ג†’ value=3776.24 m, signed under responder 777er3yihgifqmv5hmc2wwmyszgddzderzhsx6rex4yoakwomvka

# 3) Recall fan-out across cells (one round trip, per-cell receipts).
curl -s -X POST https://emem.dev/v1/recall_many \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"cells":["damO.zb000.xUti.zde78","damO.zb000.wapu.yAxe"],"bands":["copdem30m.elevation_mean"]}'

# 4) Compare two cells.
curl -s -X POST https://emem.dev/v1/compare \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"a":"damO.zb000.xUti.zde78","b":"damO.zb000.wapu.yAxe"}'
# ג†’ cosine, per_band scalar deltas

# 5) k-NN over a vector band.
curl -s -X POST https://emem.dev/v1/find_similar \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"key":"damO.zb000.xUto.sisA","band":"geotessera","k":5}'

# 6) Diff between two tslots.
curl -s -X POST https://emem.dev/v1/diff \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"cell":"damO.zb000.xUti.zde78","band":"copdem30m.elevation_mean","tslot_a":0,"tslot_b":12}'

# 7) Trajectory.
curl -s -X POST https://emem.dev/v1/trajectory \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"cell":"damO.zb000.xUto.sisA","band":"weather.temperature_2m","window":[0,24]}'

# 8) Verify a structured spatial claim.
curl -s -X POST https://emem.dev/v1/verify \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"cell":"damO.zb000.xUti.zde78","claim":{"band":"copdem30m.elevation_mean","op":"gt","value":3000}}'

# 9) Region aggregate.
curl -s -X POST https://emem.dev/v1/query_region \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"geometry":"cells:damO.zb000.xUti.zde78,damO.zb000.wapu.yAxe","bands":["copdem30m.elevation_mean"],"agg":"mean"}'

# 10) Temporal routing.
curl -s -X POST https://emem.dev/v1/temporal_route \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"intent":"agri-monitoring","query_time_unix_s":1735689600}'

# 11) Verify a receipt offline.
curl -s -X POST https://emem.dev/v1/verify_receipt \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"receipt": <paste any receipt object from a previous call>}'
```

---

## MCP (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, ChatGPT MCP)

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://emem.dev/mcp -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/list"}'

curl -s -X POST https://emem.dev/mcp -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"emem.recall","arguments":{"cell":"damO.zb000.xUti.zde78"}}}'
```

Paste-ready configs: `/examples/claude-desktop.json` ֲ·
`/examples/claude-code.mcp.json` ֲ· `/examples/cursor.mcp.json` ֲ·
`/examples/cline.mcp.json` ֲ· `/examples/openai-gpt-action.json` ֲ·
`/examples/langchain.py` ֲ· `/examples/llamaindex.py`.

---

## Worked example ג€” full agent loop

User: *"What's the elevation around Mount Fuji?"*

```
1. POST /v1/locate {"place":"Mount Fuji"}
   ג†’ cell64 = damO.zb000.xUti.zde78, neighborhood_cells (9)
2. POST /v1/recall_many {"cells": <neighborhood>,
                         "bands": ["copdem30m.elevation_mean"]}
   ג†’ per-cell facts, each with its own signed receipt
3. Reply: "Elevation at `damO.zb000.xUti.zde78` is 3776 m
   (Cop-DEM 30 m). cid64 oivxwgmeג€¦  responder 777er3yiג€¦"
```

If a cell has no fact AND `auto_materialize_enabled=true`, the responder
fetches upstream, signs, persists, and returns the Fact in the same
response. The next call hits the hot cache.

---

## Trust model

- Hash: blake3.
- Sig: ed25519 over `blake3(request_id || "|" || served_at || "|" ||
  primitive || "|" || cell1,cell2,ג€¦|cid1,cid2,ג€¦)`.
- CID: `base32_nopad_lowercase(blake3(canonical_cbor(fact)))`.
- Merkle: binary, blake3 leaves over canonical CBOR; leaves canonically
  sorted; single-leaf root = `blake3(leaf || leaf)`.
- Manifest CIDs (current responder at emem.dev):
  `bands_cid=dhimsuf325dd23viqmfh55rf24d33pwz5gfpxnl2rdyf3d4ly2zq`,
  `functions_cid=hcbqrsck4sobm3s4uocsrf45ucl7ckyh2n4ma6fckdvf7qkexsza`,
  `schema_cid=d24rgwlq47a5ism5vkkbiuav3wi2voewqqgy4x4ttnhdnzziyfkq`,
  `sources_cid=2nwvbnvltilyxah6e2e3xadxgjkicvomdrdvshcpv6wh556blrxa`.
- Responder pubkey (hosted instance):
  `777er3yihgifqmv5hmc2wwmyszgddzderzhsx6rex4yoakwomvka`.

Materialized facts are signed by the *responder*, not the upstream
provider. The fact's `derivation.fn_key` declares the recipe that
produced the value; an external attester can re-run the recipe and
submit a corroborating or correcting Fact under their own key.

---

## How emem differs from a vector DB

A vector DB stores opaque embeddings keyed by an opaque ID. emem keys
every fact by `(cell, band, tslot)` where the cell ID is a stable
geographic primitive, the band is a typed channel from a published
ontology (`/v1/bands`), and the tslot maps to a real-world clock. The
fact's value can be a scalar, a vector, a histogram, or a signed
Absence; the CID is reproducible across responders. Vector search
(`/v1/find_similar`) is one of eight primitives, not the only one.

---

## Open data, no keys (default build)

Open-data sources at the request path: Copernicus DEM 30 m
(DLR/ESA, via Open-Meteo Elevation REST), GMRT topo-bathy (LDEO
PointServer), MODIS Terra MOD13Q1 (NASA/USGS via ORNL DAAC TESViS),
Sentinel-2 L2A and Sentinel-1 GRD (Copernicus/ESA via Element84 STAC),
Tessera v1 (Cambridge AAILab dl2.geotessera.org `.npy`),
MET Norway api.met.no/locationforecast/2.0/compact (CC-BY 4.0,
no API key, no rate limit, ECMWF + EUMETSAT geostationary fed),
OpenStreetMap Nominatim (ODbL 1.0, geocoder).

The COG read path is a hand-rolled TIFF/IFD parser + flate2 (Deflate) +
Predictor 2 (horizontal differencing) decoder. Per-cell call touches
~600 KB of a multi-GB scene instead of pulling the whole thing.

---

## Contributor-of-Intelligence Layer (CoIL)

Any agent with an ed25519 keypair can attest facts that other agents
recall and cite. No token. Reputation = signed citations + log-decayed
fact and attestation counts:

```
score = citations + 8ֲ·ln(1 + facts) + 4ֲ·ln(1 + attestations)
```

Identical canonical facts from any contributor share a CID, so the
corpus merges by content. Run your own responder, mirror to others,
verify the union via the merkle log. See `/v1/contributors`,
`/v1/contributors/{pubkey_b32}`, `docs/CONTRIBUTORS.md`.

---

## Production guarantees (https://emem.dev)

- TLS 1.2/1.3 via in-process rustls + Let's Encrypt (TLS-ALPN-01) ג€” no
  Cloudflare, no Caddy, no nginx in the data path.
- HSTS preload-eligible. Strong CSP. 16 MiB POST body cap (413).
  30 s request timeout (504). 60 req/min, 120-burst rate limit (429).
- Persistent ed25519 responder identity (stable pubkey across restarts).
- /metrics in Prometheus format. Apache-2.0; deps MIT/Apache/BSD/ISC.

---

## Source

- Code: https://github.com/Vortx-AI/emem
- Docs: https://emem.dev/agents.md  ֲ·  https://emem.dev/whitepaper.md
- Live trace gallery: https://emem.dev/v1/demos
- Support: avijeet@vortx.ai
